steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
I need help setting up a git repository for tracking configuration files on my system. Here's what I need you to do:

1. Initialize a new git repository in the directory `/home/user/config-tracking`

2. Create a configuration file called `app.conf` inside that directory with the following exact content:
```
[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
name=production_db

[logging]
level=INFO
path=/var/log/app.log
```

3. Add this configuration file to git staging and make an initial commit with the exact commit message: `Initial configuration setup`

4. After completing these steps, create a log file at `/home/user/config-tracking/setup.log` that contains the following information in this exact format:
   - Line 1: The full path to the git repository
   - Line 2: The name of the tracked configuration file
   - Line 3: The commit hash (full 40-character SHA) of the initial commit
   - Line 4: The exact commit message used

Each piece of information should be on its own line with no extra whitespace or formatting. The log file should have exactly 4 lines.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user#

Analysis: I'm currently in the /home/user directory as root. I need to: 1) Create and initialize a git repository at /home/user/config-tracking, 2) Create an app.conf file with specific content, 3) Add and commit the file with a specific message, and 4) Create a setup.log file with repository information.
Plan: First, I'll create the config-tracking directory and initialize it as a git repository. Then I'll create the app.conf file with the required content using a heredoc or echo commands. After that, I'll stage the file and commit it with the specified message. Finally, I'll gather the required information (repo path, filename, commit hash, commit message) and write them to setup.log.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > app.conf << 'EOF'
[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
name=production_db

[logging]
level=INFO
path=/var/log/app.log
EOF

results.content:
Previous response had warnings:
WARNINGS: - Extra text detected before JSON object

New Terminal Output:

root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user# mkdir -p /home/user/config-tracking
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user# cd /home/user/config-tracking
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# git init
hint: Using 'master' as the name for the initial branch. This default branch name
hint: is subject to change. To configure the initial branch name to use in all
hint: of your new repositories, which will suppress this warning, call:
hint:
hint:   git config --global init.defaultBranch <name>
hint:
hint: Names commonly chosen instead of 'master' are 'main', 'trunk' and
hint: 'development'. The just-created branch can be renamed via this command:
hint:
hint:   git branch -m <name>
Initialized empty Git repository in /home/user/config-tracking/.git/
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# cat > app.conf << 'EOF'
> [database]
> host=localhost
> port=5432
> name=production_db
>
> [logging]
> level=INFO
> path=/var/log/app.log
> EOF
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# cat app.conf
[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
name=production_db

[logging]
level=INFO
path=/var/log/app.log
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking#

steps.message:
Analysis: Good progress! The git repository has been initialized in /home/user/config-tracking, and the app.conf file has been created with the correct content. Now I need to: 1) Configure git user info (required for commits), 2) Add the file to staging, 3) Commit with the exact message 'Initial configuration setup', and 4) Create the setup.log file with the required information.
Plan: First, I'll configure git user name and email (required for making commits). Then I'll add app.conf to staging and commit it with the exact message specified. After that, I'll gather the information needed for setup.log: the full repo path, the filename, the full commit hash, and the commit message. I'll write these to setup.log in the exact format required.

results.content:
New Terminal Output:

root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# git config user.email 'user@example.com'
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# git config user.name 'User'
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# git add app.conf
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# git commit -m 'Initial configuration setup'
[master (root-commit) 9847188] Initial configuration setup
 1 file changed, 8 insertions(+)
 create mode 100644 app.conf
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# pwd > setup.log
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# echo 'app.conf' >> setup.log
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# git rev-parse HEAD >> setup.log
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# echo 'Initial configuration setup' >> setup.log
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking# cat setup.log
/home/user/config-tracking
app.conf
9847188471a894511445dbff78442da7e58c3923
Initial configuration setup
root@5bbb05d0bbd3:/home/user/config-tracking#

steps.message:
Analysis: Perfect! All tasks have been completed successfully. The git repository has been initialized at /home/user/config-tracking, the app.conf file has been created with the correct content, it has been committed with the exact message 'Initial configuration setup', and the setup.log file has been created with all the required information in the correct format: Line 1 shows the full path to the repository, Line 2 shows the configuration filename, Line 3 shows the full 40-character commit hash, and Line 4 shows the exact commit message.
Plan: Let me verify that everything is correct by checking the git log and confirming the file structure one more time to ensure all requirements have been met.

steps.message:
Analysis: All requirements have been successfully completed and verified: 1) Git repository initialized at /home/user/config-tracking (confirmed by .git directory), 2) app.conf file created with the exact content specified (99 bytes, visible in ls output), 3) File added to git and committed with the exact message 'Initial configuration setup' (confirmed by git log), 4) setup.log created with exactly 4 lines containing: the full repository path, the configuration filename, the full 40-character commit hash (9847188471a894511445dbff78442da7e58c3923), and the exact commit message. All requirements have been met.
Plan: The task is complete. All steps have been successfully executed and verified.
